sections.heading: Definition
sections.body:
The `text` operator performs a full-text search using the [Process Data with Analyzers](analyzers-ref) that you specify in the [index definition](index-config-example). If you omit an analyzer, the `text` operator
uses the default [standard analyzer](ref-standard-analyzer) analyzer.

sections.heading: Syntax
Options
sections.body:
`text` uses the following terms to construct a query:

| Field               | Type                       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Necessity             |
| ------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| query               | string or array of strings | The string or strings to search for. If there are multiple terms in a string, MongoDB Search also looks for a match for each term in the string separately.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Required              |
| path                | string or array of strings | The indexed field or fields to search. You can also specify a wildcard path to search.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Required              |
| fuzzy               | document                   | Enable fuzzy search. Find strings which are similar to the search term or terms. You can't use fuzzy with synonyms.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Optional              |
| fuzzy.maxEdits      | integer                    | Maximum number of single-character edits required to match the specified search term. Value can be 1 or 2. The default value is 2. Uses Damerau-Levenshtein distance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Optional              |
| fuzzy.prefixLength  | integer                    | Number of characters at the beginning of each term in the result that must exactly match. The default value is 0.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Optional              |
| fuzzy.maxExpansions | integer                    | Maximum number of variations to generate and search for. This limit applies on a per-token basis. The default value is 50.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Optional              |
| matchCriteria       | string                     | Criteria to use to match the terms in the query. If omitted, defaults to any. Value can be one of the following:any - to return documents that contain any of the terms from the query field.all - to only return documents that contain all of the terms from the query field.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Optional, Recommended |
| score               | document                   | Score assigned to matching search term results. Use one of the following options to modify the score:boost: multiply the result score by the given number.constant: replace the result score with the given number.function: replace the result score using the given expression.When querying array values, MongoDB Search assigns higher scores if more values in the array match the query.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Optional              |
| synonyms            | string                     | Required for running queries using synonyms.Name of the synonym mapping definition in the index definition. Value can't be an empty string. You can't use fuzzy with synonyms.IMPORTANT: To use the synonyms option, you must index the query field as type string with the indexOptions property set to positions or the default value, offsets. To learn more, see Configure String Field Properties.Always use matchCriteria when using synonyms. For text queries that use synonyms without matchCriteria, MongoDB Search only matches documents where all the terms are exactly in the same position as the query. This behavior might change in the future and your query results might also change.The amount of time that MongoDB Search takes to execute queries that use synonym mappings depends on the number and size of documents in the synonym source collection. A query that uses a synonym mapping that is based on very few synonym documents might be faster than a query that uses a synonym mapping that is based on many synonym documents. | Optional              |

sections.heading: Scoring Behavior
sections.body:
By default, the text operator uses the `bm25` similarity
algorithm to score documents depending on their relevance to the query.

You can change which similarity algorithm is used by specifying a
different similarity algorithm in the `similarity.type` property for
the string fields in your MongoDB Search index definition. To learn how to
configure a MongoDB Search index for the string type, see
How to Index String Fields.

To learn more about the supported similarity algorithms, see
[Score Details](fts-similarity-algorithms).

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.body:
For `text` queries that use `synonyms` without `matchCriteria`,
MongoDB Search only matches documents which contain an exact match of the query terms
(with or without the expanded synonyms).

This behavior might change in the future and your query results might
also change. To avoid service disruptions, always use `matchCriteria`
when using `synonyms`. Or, use synonyms with the [Phrase](phrase-ref)
operator.

For example, suppose equivalent synonym mappings for `nyc` and `new
york city`. A search for `i like nyc` returns the following:

| Operator Specification | Default Behavior                           | "matchCriteria": "any"                      | "matchCriteria": "all"                        |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| text without synonyms  | Matches i OR like OR nyc                   | Matches i OR like OR nyc                    | Matches i AND like AND nyc                    |
| text with synonyms     | Matches i like nyc OR i like new york city | Matches i OR like OR (nyc OR new york city) | Matches i AND like AND (nyc OR new york city) |

The amount of time that MongoDB Search takes to execute queries that use
[Synonym](synonyms-ref) depends on the number and size of
documents in the synonym source collection. A query that uses a synonym
mapping that is based on very few synonym documents might be faster than
a query that uses a synonym mapping that is based on many synonym
documents.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.body:
The examples in this page use the `movies` collection in the
`sample_mflix` database. After loading the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets) into your cluster, create the MongoDB Search
index with dynamic mappings and run the example queries on your
cluster. You must also define a `synonyms` mapping collection in the
index as shown in the [Index Definition Examples](synonyms-index-definition-ex) to try the
[Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) examples below.

sections.heading: Basic Example
Metadata Example
Fuzzy Examples
Match all
Synonyms Examples
sections.body:
The following examples use the `text` operator to search the
`plot` field in the `sample_mflix.movies` namespace. MongoDB Search returns
results based on the [Synonym](synonyms-ref) in the
[properly formatted](synonyms-coll-spec),
`synonymous_terms`, specified in the synonym mapping definition
of the index for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection.
